No Poverty.reason: No explicit or implicit evidence in the course syllabus supports contributions to eradicating poverty.

Zero Hunger.reason: No explicit or implicit evidence in the course syllabus supports contributions to ending hunger or improving food security.

Good Health and Well-being.reason: No explicit or implicit evidence in the course syllabus supports contributions to health or well-being outcomes.

Quality Education.reason: As a university course, it provides quality tertiary education (Target 4.3). It develops professional communication skills and fosters learner autonomy, relevant for employment and entrepreneurship (Target 4.4).
evidence: Business Writing and Oral Training II
C. 溝通能力(Students will acquire professional communication skills.)
develop the skills to express their ideas and opinions logically and structurally

Gender Equality.reason: No explicit or implicit evidence in the course syllabus supports contributions to gender equality.

Clean Water and Sanitation.reason: No explicit or implicit evidence in the course syllabus supports contributions to water or sanitation.

Affordable and Clean Energy.reason: No explicit or implicit evidence in the course syllabus supports contributions to affordable or clean energy.

Decent Work and Economic Growth.reason: The course directly promotes entrepreneurship and creativity for job creation (Target 8.3) through topics like 'Be An Entrepreneur' and 'Turning Problems Into Business Ideas'.

Industry, Innovation and Infrastructure.reason: The course encourages innovation (Target 9.5) through topics like 'The World of AI' and 'Turning Problems Into Business Ideas,' fostering creative problem-solving.

Reduced Inequalities.reason: No explicit or implicit evidence in the course syllabus supports contributions to reducing inequalities.

Sustainable Cities and Communities.reason: No explicit or implicit evidence in the course syllabus supports contributions to sustainable cities or communities.

Responsible Consumption and Production.reason: Core Competence B states students will 'understand and be able to identify ethics, social responsibility, and sustainability issues,' directly supporting awareness for sustainable development (Target 12.8).
evidence: B. 理解與辨別道德、社會責任與永續議題(Students will understand and be able to identify ethics, social responsibility, and sustainability issues.)

Climate Action.reason: No explicit or implicit evidence in the course syllabus directly supports contributions to climate action.

Life Below Water.reason: No explicit or implicit evidence in the course syllabus supports contributions to marine life or ocean health.

Life on Land.reason: No explicit or implicit evidence in the course syllabus supports contributions to terrestrial ecosystems or biodiversity.

Peace, Justice and Strong Institutions.reason: The course includes a weekly topic on 'Peace' in observance of '228 Peace Memorial Day,' tangentially supporting the promotion of peaceful societies (Target 16.1).

Partnerships for the Goals.reason: While teamwork is mentioned, no evidence suggests the course fosters partnerships for global sustainable development goals.
